message:
fix(ft-autograd, ft-api): scatter (non-add) backward through src

Same severed-autograd pattern ScatterAdd had until 56c5165: ft-autograd's
Scatter op only flowed gradients to its destination buffer, dropping
the gradient that should reach src. Any composition that put a tracked
tensor into src and ran backward would silently zero src's gradient,
violating the deterministic autograd correctness contract.

ft-autograd:
  - TensorNodeOp::Scatter gains a `src: TensorNodeId` field so the
    tape remembers both inputs.
  - tape.scatter() takes the src node and OR's its requires_grad into
    the output (output depends on both args).
  - Backward now does the right two-prong accumulation:
      grad_input = incoming with overwritten positions zeroed
                   (those slots no longer hold the original value)
      grad_src   = gather(incoming, dim, index)
                   (each src[j] was copied to output[index[j]], so
                    dL/d(src[j]) = dL/d(output[index[j]]))
    Reuses ft-kernel-cpu::gather_tensor_contiguous_f64 — the same
    primitive ScatterAdd's backward already uses.
  - Both reverse-mode planning walks (visit-order traversal and
    pending-count phase) now recurse through src for both Scatter
    and ScatterAdd via a shared match arm, so the planner reaches
    src on its way back from the loss.

ft-api:
  - tensor_scatter forwards the src TensorNodeId to tape.scatter
    (it had the node in scope but was previously dropping it after
    extracting src values).

Tests:
  scatter_backward_propagates_to_src — minimal repro mirroring the
    existing scatter_add_backward_propagates_to_src test: tracked
    src, sum loss, expect grad_src == ones (no double-counting since
    the test uses unique indices).
  scatter_backward_propagates_to_input_and_src — both prongs at once,
    with overwriting indices [0,2] into a length-3 input. Verifies
    grad_input = [0,1,0] (only the un-overwritten position 1 survives)
    and grad_src = [1,1] (each src position contributed exactly once).

Workspace tests green (315 ft-autograd, 1095 ft-api, 503 ft-nn);
clippy clean on touched crates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
